name: Red Beans and Rice with Andouille Sausage
description: A Louisiana Monday-night classic — smoky, hearty, and even better reheated the next day.
recipeIngredient: 1 pound dried red kidney beans (or 2 cans, drained)
12 ounces andouille sausage, sliced into rounds
recipeInstructions: Soak beans (if dried): If using dried beans, soak 1 pound dried red kidney beans (or 2 cans, drained) in water overnight or do a quick soak: cover with water, boil 2 min, then let sit 1 hour. Drain. Skip if using canned beans.

Brown the sausage: In a large heavy pot or Dutch oven over medium-high heat, brown 12 ounces andouille sausage, sliced into rounds until slightly crispy, about 3–4 min. Remove and set aside, leaving the drippings in the pot.

Sauté the trinity: Add 1 yellow onion, diced, 1 green bell pepper, diced, and 2 celery stalks, diced to the pot. Cook over medium heat until softened, about 5 min. Add 4 garlic cloves, minced and cook 1 more minute.

Simmer the beans: Add beans, browned sausage, 4 cups chicken broth, 2 bay leaves, 1.5 teaspoons Cajun seasoning, 1 teaspoon smoked paprika, and 0.5 teaspoon dried thyme. Bring to a boil, then reduce heat and simmer uncovered for 1.5–2 hours (or 45 min for canned beans), stirring occasionally, until beans are very tender and creamy.
Smash and season: Remove bay leaves. Use the back of a spoon to smash about a quarter of the beans against the pot — this thickens the sauce naturally. Season with salt and pepper to taste.
Cook the rice: Cook 2 cups long-grain white rice according to package directions. Serve beans over rice, topped with sliced green onions for garnish.
